unit.subject: English
unit.unitOverview: In this unit, your child learns the words for games and play — action words like bounce, roll, throw and catch. They learn the names of parts of the body, and words that say where things are (on, under, next to), and left and right. They play simple games, learn a game from another country, and listen to a funny story. Along the way they hear the short 'u' sound (sun, fun, duck). The goal is happy, active English — learning through play.
unit.learningPath:
Begin with the guided teacher launch and learn a few words through pictures and play.
Listen to the Unit story and join in with repeated language.
Complete six language, speaking, early-writing and practical activities.
Meet your teacher three times each week for two weeks.
Finish the ten-question checkpoint and tell an adult what you can do.

visual.alt: Child-friendly illustration for Fun and Games

readings.title: Amal and the Big Ball
readings.theme: movement, body parts, position and fair play
readings.setting: Home, online lesson or Grade 1 classroom
readings.passageScript:
It was a sunny day in the town of Hargeisa. Amal ran outside to play. She had a big red ball.

“Look at my ball!” said Amal. She could bounce it. Bounce, bounce, bounce!

Her friend Samira came to play. “Can you catch the ball?” asked Amal.

“Yes, I can!” said Samira. Amal did throw the ball. Samira did catch it. “Well done!” said Amal.

Then little Ishmael came. He was only four. “Can I play too?” asked Ishmael.

Selma looked at the ball. She wanted to play with Samira. But Ishmael looked sad.

“Yes!” said Amal. “Let us all play. We can take turns.”

Selma did roll the ball to Ishmael. Roll, roll, roll! Ishmael put his two hands on the ball. He was happy.

“Now throw it to me!” said Samira. Ishmael did throw the ball. But — oh no! The ball went up, up, up!

The ball went into a big tree. It was on a branch. “My ball is stuck!” said Amal.

The three friends looked up. How could they get the ball?

“I know!” said Samira. “Let us jump!” They all did jump. Jump, jump, jump! But the ball was too high.

Just then, big brother Adam came. He was tall. “I can help,” said Adam.

Adam did shake the branch. Shake, shake, shake! The ball came down. Amal did catch it. “Thank you, Adam!” said all the children.

“You shared the ball,” said Adam. “You are good friends.”

Selma, Samira and Ishmael played all day. They did bounce and roll and catch. It was so much fun!

When the sun went down, Amal said, “Sharing is the best game of all.”

readings.title: Talk about Fun and Games
readings.theme: movement, body parts, position and fair play
readings.setting: Home, online lesson or Grade 1 classroom
readings.passageScript: Look, point and talk. I can ___. Can you ___? This is my ___. The ball is on the ___. Touch your ___. I am ___ing. An adult reads each line while the learner points, repeats and acts.

readings.title: Fun and Games rhyme
readings.theme: movement, body parts, position and fair play
readings.setting: Home, online lesson or Grade 1 classroom
readings.passageScript:
The “Bounce the Ball” poem — say it together
Bounce the ball,  /  Ball, ball,  /  Bounce the ball!  /  Roll it, throw it,  /  Catch the ball!
Clap the rhythm as you say it. If you have a ball, bounce it as you read. Say it three times — a little faster each time!
The “Wind the Bobbin Up” action song
Wind the bobbin up.  /  Wind the bobbin up.  /  Pull, pull. Clap, clap, clap.  /  Point to the ceiling.  /  Point to the floor.  /  Point to the window and point to the door.  /  Clap your hands together, 1, 2, 3.  /  Put your hands upon your knees.
Do every action as you sing. Point to the ceiling, the floor, the window and the door. This is a wonderful way to learn body and action words with your whole body.
Little rhyme: “I can run like a puppy” (short ‘u’ sound)
I can run like a puppy.  /  I can jump like a bug.  /  I can buzz like a bee.  /  I can sit on the rug.

grammar.title: Language pattern 1
grammar.explanation: Listen to the model, point to the matching picture, then say the pattern: I can ___.
grammar.commonMistake: A Grade 1 learner may answer with one word. Model the whole short pattern warmly and let the learner try again.
grammar.memoryTip: Say it, tap it, then build it with word cards.
grammar.practice: Complete and say three examples using: I can ___.

speaking.title: Speaking 1 - Listen and point
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: I can ___. An adult models once, the learner repeats, then changes one word using a picture or real object.
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern I can ___. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.title: Speaking 2 - Repeat the model
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: Can you ___? An adult models once, the learner repeats, then changes one word using a picture or real object.
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern Can you ___? Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern This is my ___. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern The ball is on the ___. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.title: Speaking 5 - Role-play

speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern Touch your ___. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.title: Speaking 6 - My confident turn

speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern I am ___ing. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

writing.title: Writing 1 - Draw and tell
writing.promptAndInstructions: Draw one idea about movement, body parts, position and fair play. Tell an adult what it shows.
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 2 - Trace
writing.promptAndInstructions: Trace two useful words, saying each sound or word as you trace.
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 3 - Match and copy

writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 4 - Label
writing.promptAndInstructions: Add two labels to your drawing or picture.
writing.expectedLength: One label, phrase or short sentence
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 5 - Complete a pattern
writing.expectedLength: One label, phrase or short sentence
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 6 - Create one sentence
writing.promptAndInstructions: Choose a picture and complete one short sentence using: I am ___ing.

writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult

activities.title: Activity 1
activities.instructionsAndItems: 1.  Draw your favourite game or toy. Then say: “I like to play with my ______.”
activities.answerSummary: Accept the correct match, action, picture choice or a relevant supported spoken response.
activities.deliveryMode: Shared online UI with adult or teacher support

activities.title: Activity 2
activities.instructionsAndItems: 2.  Colour the ball. Then do the action! Bounce, roll and catch a real ball or a pretend one.

liveSessions.title: Meet the words
liveSessions.beforeSession: Bring crayons and one real object connected to the Unit.
liveSessions.agenda: 5 min hello song; 7 min model and repeat; 8 min game or story; 7 min guided practice; 3 min celebrate and preview
liveSessions.afterSession: Show one word, action, drawing or sentence from Fun and Games to an adult.

liveSessions.title: Story and talk
liveSessions.beforeSession: Complete the previous short practice with an adult.

teacherNotes.note: Use 15-20 minute independent blocks inside the lesson. Read every instruction aloud, model before expecting a response, and accept pointing, acting, drawing, tracing or speaking as valid evidence.
A teacher or caregiver remains present whenever the young learner uses recording or AI tutor features. The adult controls the device and protects the child's personal information.

selfAssessment.statement: I can name and act out ball actions (bounce, roll, throw, catch, hit).
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

selfAssessment.statement: I can name parts of the body (hand, foot, ear, nose, shoulder, head…).
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

selfAssessment.statement: I can say where something is (on, under, next to) and show left and right.
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

selfAssessment.statement: I can ask and answer “Can you ___?” with “Yes, I can.” / “No, I can't.”.
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

selfAssessment.statement: I can follow simple game instructions and take turns fairly.
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

selfAssessment.statement: I can hear and say words with the short ‘u’ sound (sun, fun, duck, bug).
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself
